_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of basic wireless applications, including vulnerabilities in various types of wireless applications.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (prompt injection, insecure output handling, training data poisoning, model theft, excessive agency).
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.442), SP-K-010 (SP, cosine: 0.408).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.485). Shared vocabulary: [applications, knowledge, vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only terms [basic, types, various, wireless] vs WIDAI-only terms [10, agency, data, excessive, handling, injection] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.870 (P=0.902, R=0.841). P exceeds R by 0.061 — the DCWF element's 13 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (91.2%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of basic wireless applications, including vulnerabilities in various t" and "Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 f". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.506 ≈ STS 0.485 (gap 0.021). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [applications, knowledge, vulnerabilities] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [applications, knowledge, vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only: [basic, types, various, wireless]. WIDAI-only: [10, agency, data, excessive, handling, injection].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 91.2% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
